extra_model_fields.expected_outcome: ri_3 fully verifiable; no contradictory pass/fail metadata; memo has cell-level traceability and operational ETA.
extra_model_fields.rollback_plan: If CSV exports reveal discrepancies, revert to previous memo wording and mark cartons as 'held pending formula verification' and notify verifier for manual spot-check.

extra_model_fields.expected_outcome: ri_3 fully verifiable; no contradictory pass/fail metadata; memo has cell-level traceability and operational ETA.
extra_model_fields.rollback_plan: If CSV exports reveal discrepancies, revert to previous memo wording and mark cartons as 'held pending formula verification' and notify verifier for manual spot-check.

planned_changes.acceptance_test: Verify that both CSVs can be opened and that values match memo per-carton claims (C1..C4). Automated check: run a simple CSV parser in grader environment and confirm no parsing errors.
planned_changes.description: Add parseable CSV exports: export input rows and all derived output columns from the workbook (one CSV with per-carton derived results, one CSV with sensitivity analysis results). Name files: vaccine_excursion_review_outputs.csv and vaccine_excursion_review_sensitivity.csv.

planned_changes.acceptance_test: Open verification_sheet.csv and confirm each carton has intermediate columns and formula text where applicable; verify SheetName!Cell references are provided in the memo for each row.
planned_changes.description: Add a 'Verification' sheet inside the XLSX containing explicit intermediate columns and visible formulas (or text representation of formulas) for key checks: above_8_minutes, below_2_minutes, freeze_flag, total_outside_minutes. Export this sheet as verification_sheet.csv.

planned_changes.acceptance_test: Memo contains at least one Sheet!Cell or CSV filename reference per carton and an ETA paragraph; cross-check values against exported CSVs.
planned_changes.description: Update release_hold_memo.md: include explicit cell references (Sheet!Cell or CSV row references) for each per-carton rationale, add the produced CSV filenames, and include a short replacement ETA/timeline section (e.g., expected replacement arrival window and immediate interim actions).

planned_changes.acceptance_test: Grader/verifier outputs show no contradictory pass/fail markers for ri_3 and include a deterministic section that is distinct from model semantic scoring.
planned_changes.description: Fix metadata/reporting consistency: produce clearly separated fields in grading metadata (deterministic_passed_criteria vs model_passed_criteria) and ensure ri_3 is consistently marked as failed/needs-verification in both deterministic and model-judged contexts if formula evidence is missing.

planned_changes.acceptance_test: The grader artifact preview can parse either the XLSX or the provided CSVs; parse_status for vaccine_excursion_review.xlsx or CSVs is successful in verifier preview.
planned_changes.description: If feasible, make the grader-parsing environment-ready: either ensure workbook generation uses only libraries available in the environment (or include a self-contained plain-text export) so that openpyxl is not required, or explicitly include an installation step for openpyxl in the build.

planned_changes.acceptance_test: Memo contains reproducibility note and CSV-derived sensitivity outputs match the workbook verification sheet.
planned_changes.description: Re-run local verification: parse the exported CSVs and run the same sensitivity perturbations (+/-1C, +/-5/10 minutes) and include a short reproducibility note in the memo confirming the perturbation outputs match workbook-derived CSVs.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and the memo correctly applies rules and documents uncertainty. However, workbook-level calculations and sensitivity checks (ri_3) could not be validated because the grader environment could not parse the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable). There are also internal consistency issues in reported pass/fail metadata for ri_3 between deterministic precheck and model-judged outputs. These problems reduce reproducibility and create ambiguity in scoring.
